item.title: Secrets CLI — add and list commands
steps: Create src/cli/secrets.ts with `secrets` subcommand and `add`/`list` sub-subcommands
Implement `secrets add <name>`: prompt for value with masked input (no echo), write to .env via existing parseEnv/serializeEnv in src/security/secrets/env.ts, enforce chmod 600
Add optional `--validate` flag to `secrets add` that runs the matching credential health probe from src/security/credentials/index.ts after writing

Implement `secrets list`: read .env, display table with columns: name, provider_category (inferred from key name patterns), health_status (via credential probes), age (from metadata), last_rotated — never display values
Create src/security/secrets/metadata.ts to track secret metadata (created_at, rotated_at, provider_category) in a sidecar JSON file (.env.meta) with 600 permissions
Add SecretEntry type definition in src/security/secrets/types.ts matching PRODUCT.md data model
Write unit tests for add flow (masked input mock, .env write, permission enforcement) and list display formatting
acceptanceCriteria: `clawhq secrets add MY_KEY` prompts for masked input and writes to .env with 600 permissions
`clawhq secrets add MY_KEY --validate` runs health probe after writing and reports valid/invalid
`clawhq secrets list` shows all secrets with name, category, health, age — never shows values
Secret metadata (.env.meta) tracks created_at and provider_category
Unit tests cover .env write, metadata tracking, and list formatting
